methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: Native ETH held transiently in module contracts during wrap/unwrap flows (msg.value) and potentially in Proxy.receive()/fallback()
Wrapped native token (WETH) held by protocol core (the contract that owns PoolStorage via StorageSlot STORAGE_POSITION_POOL)
All ERC20 underlying tokens configured in pools (liquidity held by protocol core address; tracked by AssetData.availableLiquidity, totalScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply and per-user scaled supplies)
All ERC721 tokens deposited as collateral (held by protocol core address; tracked by AssetData.erc721TokenData[tokenId], totalScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply and per-user counts)
Isolated-loan auction escrow bid amounts in ERC20 (tracked by AssetData.totalBidAmout; custody in protocol core address)
Accrued protocol fees (tracked by AssetData.accruedFee; later minted as supply shares to treasury via executeCollectFeeToTreasury)
Borrow positions / debt principal+interest represented as scaled borrows in GroupData (totalScaledCrossBorrow/IsolateBorrow and per-user scaled borrows) and IsolateLoanData.scaledAmount
Flash-loaned ERC20 and ERC721 assets temporarily transferred out and expected returned within transaction (no explicit fee in shown code)
Delegation rights for ERC721s via external DelegateRegistryV2 (non-financial but can affect control/utility of NFTs)
state_assets: Diamond-like modular dispatcher state: moduleLookup[moduleId] => implementation address
proxyLookup[moduleId] => Proxy address for single-proxy modules
reentrancyLock (custom reentrancy guard state in Base)
PoolStorage at fixed slot STORAGE_POSITION_POOL: {addressProvider, wrappedNativeToken, nextPoolId, poolLookup, poolList}
Per-pool PoolData: {poolId, name, isPaused, enabledGroups, groupList, assetLookup, assetList, loanLookup, accountLookup, isYieldEnabled/isYieldPaused/yieldGroup}
Per-asset AssetData config: caps, factors, flags, feeFactor, liquidation params, auctionDuration, yield caps
Per-asset accounting: totalScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply, availableLiquidity, totalBidAmout, userScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply, supplyRate/supplyIndex, accruedFee, lastUpdateTimestamp
Per-asset per-group GroupData: rateModel, totalScaledCrossBorrow/IsolateBorrow, userScaledCrossBorrow/IsolateBorrow, borrowRate/borrowIndex, groupId
Per-ERC721 token state: ERC721TokenData {owner, supplyMode, lockerAddr}
Per-isolated-loan state: IsolateLoanData {reserveAsset, scaledAmount, reserveGroup, loanStatus, bidStartTimestamp, firstBidder, lastBidder, bidAmount}
Per-account state: suppliedAssets set, borrowedAssets set, operatorApprovals[asset][operator] => bool
privilege_assets: Pool admin role (via IACLManager from AddressProvider) controlling fee collection (executeCollectFeeToTreasury)
Emergency admin role (via IACLManager) (check function exists but not used in provided code paths; likely used elsewhere)
Global pause capability inherited from OpenZeppelin Pausable in Base (whenNotPaused gates on module functions; internal _pause/_unpause exist but no external exposure in provided contracts)
Module upgrade/dispatch authority in core Storage: ability to set moduleLookup/trustedSenders/proxyLookup (not shown here, but critical)
AddressProvider-controlled external dependency pointers: ACLManager, Treasury, PriceOracle, DelegateRegistryV2 (indirect governance/admin control)
Proxy creator authority: Proxy.emergencyEtherTransfer callable only by creator (the contract that deployed the Proxy via Base._createProxy)
reputation_assets: Treasury fee share balance represented as protocol 'supplied' position minted via accruedFee collection
Lender/borrower positions and protocol solvency metrics (health factor calculations) affecting liquidation outcomes and perceived trust
Whitelist/permissioning implied by trustedSenders dispatch model (ability to impersonate msgSender via trailing calldata if misconfigured)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: BaseModule -> Base -> (Storage, OpenZeppelin Pausable, OpenZeppelin ERC721Holder). Service modules (BVault, CrossLending, CrossLiquidation, IsolateLending, IsolateLiquidation, FlashLoan) inherit BaseModule. Base composes a custom Proxy factory and delegatecall-based module invocation (callInternalModule). Pool business logic is mostly implemented in libraries (SupplyLogic, BorrowLogic, LiquidationLogic, IsolateLogic, FlashLoanLogic, PoolLogic) operating on PoolStorage located at a fixed storage slot via StorageSlot.getPoolStorage().
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin upgradeable libs: EnumerableSetUpgradeable, SafeERC20Upgradeable, IERC20Upgradeable, IERC721Upgradeable, SafeCastUpgradeable
External protocol interfaces via AddressProvider: IACLManager, IPriceOracleGetter, IDelegateRegistryV2, IInterestRateModel, IFlashLoanReceiver, IWETH
Custom helpers (not provided): Constants, Errors, Events
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: Custom modular proxy architecture: users interact with per-module contracts (BaseModule) but msgSender is derived from trailing calldata, suggesting calls are expected to be forwarded through proxy(s) that append the original sender. Base can deploy minimal Proxy instances (not EIP-1967) which forward calls back to the creator contract using a hardcoded dispatch() selector. Pool state is stored at a deterministic slot (STORAGE_POSITION_POOL) suggesting multiple modules share the same storage (facet-like). No explicit UUPS/Transparent/Beacon/Diamond(EIP-2535) implementation is present in provided files; however the architecture resembles a custom dispatcher + delegatecall module registry.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Business functions are generally permissionless for users but gated by whenNotPaused and a custom nonReentrant. Admin permission checks use ACLManager fetched from AddressProvider (checkCallerIsPoolAdmin/checkCallerIsEmergencyAdmin). Pausable's external pause/unpause are not exposed in provided contracts; only internal _pause/_unpause exist via inheritance. Proxy has a creator-only ETH rescue. Core upgrade/dispatch permissions likely exist in an unseen 'creator/core' contract that owns moduleLookup/trustedSenders and implements dispatch().
system_architecture.inventories_note: Inherited management functions: OpenZeppelin Pausable is inherited by Base, but no external/public pause()/unpause() is defined in analyzed contracts; if system relies on pausing, it must be implemented elsewhere. No Ownable/AccessControl inherited directly in analyzed modules.

external_calls: CALL to creator with calldata prefixed by dispatch() selector and appended caller address; forwards msg.value
If caller==creator: emits arbitrary logs via EVM LOG0-LOG4 based on crafted calldata

state_changes: PoolStorage/PoolData/AssetData/GroupData: updates interest indices/rates; increases groupData.totalScaledCrossBorrow and userScaledCrossBorrow; updates AccountData.borrowedAssets; decreases assetData.availableLiquidity; transfers ERC20 out
external_calls: IERC20.safeTransfer(...) from protocol core to receiver (via VaultLogic.erc20TransferOutLiquidity)
IWETH.transferFrom/user flows if asset is native (unwrapNativeTokenInWallet uses IWETH.transferFrom + IWETH.withdraw + native transfer)

state_changes: Interest indices/rates updated; decreases groupData.totalScaledCrossBorrow and userScaledCrossBorrow; updates AccountData.borrowedAssets; increases assetData.availableLiquidity; pulls ERC20 from msgSender into protocol core
external_calls: IWETH.transferFrom(address(this), user, amount) (wrapNativeTokenInWallet uses transferFrom, see defects)

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates for collateral & debt assets
Repays borrower debt by decreasing userScaledCrossBorrow across groups
Transfers debt asset in from liquidator to protocol core
Either decreases borrower supply and transfers collateral out OR transfers supply shares to liquidator
Updates AccountData.borrowedAssets and suppliedAssets flags
external_calls: IERC20.safeTransfer(protocol core -> liquidator, collateralAmount) if not supplyAsCollateral
Native wrap/unwrap via IWETH + ETH transfer if assets are native

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates
Repays borrower ERC20 debt (scaled cross borrow decreased)
Transfers ERC20 in from liquidator
Either transfers ERC721 supply ownership records to liquidator OR burns supply records and transfers NFTs out
May increase borrower cross supply of debtAsset for any 'remainDebtToLiquidate' credit
external_calls: IERC721.safeTransferFrom(protocol core -> liquidator, tokenIds) if not supplyAsCollateral
Native wrap via IWETH if debtAsset is native

state_changes: Updates debt asset interest indices/rates
Creates/updates IsolateLoanData per NFT; sets ERC721TokenData.lockerAddr=address(this) on first borrow
Increases groupData.totalScaledIsolateBorrow and userScaledIsolateBorrow (onBehalf) using scaled amounts
Decreases assetData.availableLiquidity; transfers ERC20 out to receiver
external_calls: Native unwrap via IWETH + ETH transfer if asset is native

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates
Decreases groupData.totalScaledIsolateBorrow and userScaledIsolateBorrow (onBehalf) per NFT
If full repay: clears ERC721TokenData.lockerAddr and deletes IsolateLoanData
Increases assetData.availableLiquidity; pulls ERC20 from msgSender
Updates interest indices
Moves bid amounts into escrow: increases AssetData.totalBidAmout; records loanData.firstBidder/lastBidder/bidAmount/bidStartTimestamp/loanStatus
Refunds previous bidder from escrow (erc20TransferOutBidAmount)
external_calls: Native wrap via IWETH if bidding with native

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates
Decreases isolate borrow (BUG RISK: uses params.msgSender as debtor in erc20DecreaseIsolateScaledBorrow)
Refunds last bidder from escrow; transfers bid fine from redeemer to first bidder
Resets auction fields; reduces loan scaledAmount by redeem amount
external_calls: Native wrap via IWETH if asset is native

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates
Burns isolate borrow from token owner; settles escrow: moves borrowAmount from totalBidAmout to availableLiquidity; may transfer remainder to borrower; may pull extra from liquidator
Deletes IsolateLoanData
Transfers or assigns ERC721 collateral to last bidder/liquidator depending on supplyAsCollateral
external_calls: IERC20.safeTransferFrom(liquidator, protocol core, extraAmount) if needed
IERC20.safeTransfer(protocol core -> borrower, remainBidAmount) if any
Native wrap via IWETH if asset is native

state_changes: Updates interest indices/rates
Increases user scaled cross supply and totalScaledCrossSupply
Updates AccountData.suppliedAssets
Increases assetData.availableLiquidity and pulls ERC20 from msgSender
Updates interest indices/rates
Decreases user scaled cross supply and totalScaledCrossSupply
Updates AccountData.suppliedAssets
Decreases assetData.availableLiquidity; transfers ERC20 out
Performs post-withdraw HF validation
external_calls: Native unwrap via IWETH + ETH transfer if native

state_changes: Sets ERC721TokenData.owner and supplyMode per tokenId
Updates totalScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply and userScaledCrossSupply/IsolateSupply (counts)
Increases assetData.availableLiquidity by token count
Updates AccountData.suppliedAssets
external_calls: IERC721.safeTransferFrom(msgSender, protocol core, tokenId) per token

state_changes: Clears ERC721TokenData.owner/supplyMode; updates per-user and total counts
Decreases assetData.availableLiquidity by token count
Updates AccountData.suppliedAssets for cross mode only
Validates HF for cross withdrawals; isolate withdrawals require no loan and lockerAddr==0
external_calls: IERC721.safeTransferFrom(protocol core -> receiver, tokenId) per token

state_changes: Moves ERC721TokenData.supplyMode between cross/isolate and updates user/total counts accordingly
Updates AccountData.suppliedAssets
Validates HF after mode change

For each asset: sets assetData.accruedFee=0; updates interest indices; mints treasury supply shares by increasing totalScaledCrossSupply and treasury userScaledCrossSupply; updates treasury suppliedAssets flag
external_calls: IInterestRateModel.calculateGroupBorrowRate() indirectly via updateInterestIndexs? (no, updateInterestIndexs does not call IRM; updateInterestRates not called here)

state_changes: AccountData.operatorApprovals[asset][operator]=approved for msgSender
Transfers ERC20s out and expects them returned; no direct state accounting changes to availableLiquidity in provided code
external_calls: IERC20.safeTransfer(protocol core -> receiver, amount) per asset
IERC20.safeTransferFrom(receiver, protocol core, amount) per asset

state_changes: Transfers ERC721s out and expects them returned; enforces tokenData.owner==msgSender and isolate loan status constraints

upgradeability_surface.notes: Not a standard upgrade proxy; immutable creator; fallback forwards to creator with dispatch() selector. Includes creator-only emergencyEtherTransfer. Allows creator to emit arbitrary logs via fallback when msg.sender==creator.

Facet-like shared storage across modules via fixed slot STORAGE_POSITION_POOL plus additional Storage variables (moduleLookup/proxyLookup/trustedSenders/reentrancyLock). Missing core dispatcher/upgrade manager contract in provided set; upgrade safety depends on unseen functions that mutate moduleLookup/trustedSenders.
Module contract shares global PoolStorage via StorageSlot; relies on trailing msgSender; upgradeability via module registry (unseen).
Module shares global PoolStorage via fixed slot; relies on trailing msgSender.
Module shares global PoolStorage via fixed slot; relies on trailing msgSender.
Module shares global PoolStorage via fixed slot; relies on trailing msgSender.
Module shares global PoolStorage via fixed slot; relies on trailing msgSender.
Module shares global PoolStorage via fixed slot; flashLoanERC20 intentionally omits nonReentrant; relies on receiver callback safety.
